summary: Patient Data Encryption and Audit Trail Test
description: This test case validates that patient data is encrypted using AES-256 and audit trails are stored for every database change.
objective: Validate system compliance with FDA Part 11 and ISO 9001 requirements for encryption and audit trail storage.
prerequisites: System must be configured to use AES-256 encryption
Database must be set up to store audit trails

test_steps.description: Enter a new patient record into the system
Inspect the stored patient data to verify it is encrypted with AES-256
Trigger a database change (e.g. update, delete) to test audit trail storage
Observe the system's response to verify that an audit trail is stored for the database change

expected_result: The patient data should be encrypted with AES-256 and an audit trail should be stored for every database change.

test_data.database_change: Update or delete operation on a sample database table

acceptance_criteria: Patient data is encrypted with AES-256
Audit trails are stored for every database change
